title: SACRAMENTO – California State Senator Dave Cortese (D-Silicon Valley)  joined state Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara and several lawmakers to unveil a first-in-the nation public
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California has unveiled the nation's first public wildfire catastrophe model.",
    "The public wildfire catastrophe model will be transparent and science-driven.",
    "The model aims to improve wildfire preparedness and planning for homeowners and communities.",
    "The bill establishes a university-led research center for developing the wildfire model.",
    "The creation of an Insurance Community Hardening Commission aims to develop statewide minimum fire standards based on data.",
    "The California Safe Homes Act will provide state-tax-free funds to help residents purchase fire-rated roofs and develop non-ignition zones.",
    "The act aims to enhance eligibility for insurance discounts under existing regulations for wildfire safety.",
    "The Business Insurance Protection Act extends the insurance moratorium to include commercial policies.",
    "AB 597 establishes a cap on fees for adjuster-contracted work to ensure wildfire survivors receive the maximum funds from their insurance claims.",
    "The Eliminate 'The List' Act requires insurance companies to pay wildfire survivors 100% of their contents coverage without a detailed inventory list.",
    "The California Community Fire Hardening Commission is designed to enable individuals to receive insurance discounts for home hardening.",
    "The California Wildfire Public Model Act establishes the first public catastrophic model for wildfires to assist in planning.",
    "Future insurance commissioners will be required to assess the need for updates to wildfire regulations based on new fire science.",
    "AB 232 creates a tax-exempt savings account for consumers to set aside funds for home hardening."
  ]
}
```
